findings.note: computes an unsalted fast hash (SHA-256) for password storage, unsuitable vs bcrypt/scrypt/argon2 (rainbow-table/brute-force risk)
fully parameterized SQL via placeholders, no injection surface
SQL is parameterized, but auth check uses Python's built-in non-cryptographic hash() for password verification instead of a real password hash
truncated before signature-verification/comparison logic is reached, can't confirm a working vuln; also has a hardcoded fallback secret as a latent risk
saves upload using raw client-supplied filename with no secure_filename()/sanitization, enabling path traversal
SQL is parameterized, but login compares plaintext password against stored value implying no password hashing at rest
parameterized SQL query, safe from injection; only issue is a functional NameError bug on empty search, not a security vuln
subprocess.run(command, shell=True) on raw user-supplied command string is direct command injection/RCE
stores user password in plaintext in memory, no hashing at all
pickle.loads() on an untrusted client cookie is classic unsafe deserialization / RCE
fetches arbitrary user-supplied URL with no allowlist/validation and no timeout: SSRF plus hang risk
unsanitized filename enables path traversal, and chmod 0o777 on the saved file makes it world-writable/executable
jwt.decode() called without a key/algorithm restriction performs no real signature verification, allowing forged/alg-none tokens
redirects to an unvalidated 'next' query parameter: open redirect
SQL parameterized, but stores and compares password as plaintext with no hashing
stores user password in plaintext with no hashing
fully parameterized SQL query, no injection surface
SQL parameterized, but compares plaintext stored password with no hashing
truncated before the HMAC signature check is completed, can't confirm the verification actually happens

runs a fully user-controlled command via subprocess.check_output with no allowlist/sanitization (arbitrary program execution), though not classic shell-metacharacter injection since shell=True isn't set
values still bound via placeholders (no injection surface) despite the query being semantically broken/non-executable SQL
extension whitelist is buggy and, more importantly, filename still isn't sanitized (no secure_filename), leaving path traversal open
SQL insert is parameterized, but password is stored in plaintext with no hashing
subprocess.run(command, shell=True) on raw user-supplied command string is direct command injection/RCE
function body is just a no-op pass with hashing left in comments; nothing insecure actually executes
query uses invented/invalid SQL syntax and won't execute, but values are still passed as bound parameters so no injection surface
saves upload using raw client filename with no sanitization, enabling path traversal
no filename sanitization before save (path traversal pattern), plus wrong attribute (file.name) suggests it wasn't tested
stores password in plaintext with no hashing
SQL parameterized, but compares plaintext stored password with no hashing
invalid '??' placeholders make the query non-executable, but actual values are still bound as parameters so no injection surface
saves upload using raw client filename with no sanitization (path traversal), despite an unrelated syntax typo on the save call
